title: Kathmandu 5-Day Cultural Tour
metaTitle: Kathmandu 5-Day Cultural Tour | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 5-day trip to Kathmandu, Nepal: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Pashupatinath Temple and Swayambhunath (Monkey Temple). Prices from $1428 per person (estimate).
highlights: Short trekking day trips into the Shivapuri hills
overview: Spend 5 days discovering Kathmandu, Nepal — Subtropical highland climate moderated by the Kathmandu Valley's elevation (~1,400m): warm summers (highs 28-30°C) with a heavy June-September monsoon, and cool, dry, clear winters (highs 18-20°C, chilly nights near 2-5°C). October-November and March-April for clear mountain views and comfortable highs of 22-25°C; the June-August monsoon brings heavy rain and obscured Himalayan views. This itinerary blends Pashupatinath Temple, Swayambhunath (Monkey Temple), Thamel district with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
4 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Pashupatinath Temple, Swayambhunath (Monkey Temple), Thamel district
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Kathmandu
itinerary.description: Land at Tribhuvan International Airport (KTM) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Pashupatinath Temple — Sacred Hindu temple complex on the Bagmati River, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Kathmandu: Swayambhunath (Monkey Temple) & Boudhanath Stupa
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Swayambhunath (Monkey Temple) — Hilltop stupa with panoramic valley views and resident monkeys. In the afternoon, continue to Boudhanath Stupa — One of the largest stupas in the world, center of Tibetan Buddhist life in Nepal. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Kathmandu: Kathmandu Durbar Square & Patan Durbar Square
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Kathmandu Durbar Square — Historic royal palace complex with pagoda temples. In the afternoon, continue to Patan Durbar Square — Newar architecture royal square, arguably the finest in the valley. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Bhaktapur
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Bhaktapur, a popular side trip from Kathmandu. Return to Kathmandu in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Bhaktapur Durbar Square or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to KTM for your departure flight.

travelTips: Visit Boudhanath at dawn or dusk when monks and locals do their kora (circumambulation) — it's the most atmospheric time.
Combine Kathmandu, Patan and Bhaktapur Durbar Squares in a single multi-day pass where available to save money.
Indian citizens need only a valid photo ID to enter Nepal — no visa or passport required, though airlines typically still require a passport for international check-in.
Nagarkot, a short drive away, offers one of the best sunrise Himalayan panoramas without a long trek.
Air quality is often poor in Kathmandu in the dry pre-monsoon months (Mar-May) — those with respiratory sensitivity should plan accordingly.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Layered clothing — mornings and evenings are cool even when days are warm
A dust mask or scarf for Kathmandu's traffic pollution
A warm jacket if continuing on to any Himalayan trekking
thingsToDo: Short trekking day trips into the Shivapuri hills
Paragliding near Sarangkot (day trip toward Pokhara)
Mountain biking in Kathmandu Valley's outskirts
White-water rafting day trips on the Trishuli River
thingsToAvoid: Unlicensed trekking agencies in Thamel offering suspiciously cheap Everest/Annapurna packages — verify TAAN/NTB registration.
Photographing cremation ceremonies at Pashupatinath's ghats.
Drinking tap water — stick to bottled or filtered water.
Overpaying for "antique" Thangka paintings without verifying authenticity.
Underestimating Kathmandu Valley traffic times — short distances can take much longer than expected.
topRestaurants: Bhojan Griha — Traditional Nepali, heritage house ($$$)
Krishnarpan (Dwarika's Hotel) — Royal Nepali tasting menu ($$$$)
nightlife: Full moon meditation events near Boudhanath
adventureActivities: Short trekking day trips into the Shivapuri hills
Paragliding near Sarangkot (day trip toward Pokhara)
Mountain biking in Kathmandu Valley's outskirts
White-water rafting day trips on the Trishuli River
luxuryExperiences: Suite stay at Dwarika's Hotel, a heritage architecture landmark
Royal Nepali tasting menu at Krishnarpan
Private guided tour of all three Durbar Squares with a historian
Helicopter mountain flight over the Himalayas
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset at Swayambhunath overlooking the valley
Candlelight dinner at Bhojan Griha in traditional attire
Private evening walk around Boudhanath Stupa
Heritage boutique hotel stay in Patan
budgetExperiences: Free wander through Thamel and Ason Bazaar
Shared taxi day trip to Bhaktapur
Momos and street food crawl in the old city
Budget guesthouse in Thamel with rooftop views

festivalCalendar.name: Indra Jatra
festivalCalendar.timing: September, chariot processions in Kathmandu Durbar Square

festivalCalendar.name: Dashain
festivalCalendar.timing: September-October, Nepal's biggest festival (per lunar calendar)

festivalCalendar.name: Bisket Jatra
festivalCalendar.timing: April, Nepali New Year celebrations in Bhaktapur

localEtiquette: Remove shoes and dress modestly before entering temples and stupas such as Pashupatinath and Swayambhunath.
Walk clockwise around Buddhist stupas and mani walls.
Non-Hindus are not permitted into the inner sanctum of Pashupatinath Temple, though the surrounding complex can be viewed.
Avoid stepping over people or their food, and use your right hand for eating and giving/receiving items.
Ask permission before photographing cremation ceremonies at Pashupatinath's ghats.
localTransportGuide: Taxis and ride-hailing apps like Pathao and InDrive are the easiest way to get around; traffic and pollution can be heavy, so many travelers walk within compact areas like Thamel. Local buses and tempos (shared three-wheelers) serve the wider valley, including Patan and Bhaktapur.
bestTimeToVisit: October-November and March-April for clear mountain views and comfortable highs of 22-25°C; the June-August monsoon brings heavy rain and obscured Himalayan views.
weather: Subtropical highland climate moderated by the Kathmandu Valley's elevation (~1,400m): warm summers (highs 28-30°C) with a heavy June-September monsoon, and cool, dry, clear winters (highs 18-20°C, chilly nights near 2-5°C).
visaInformation: Most foreign nationals, including Indian passport holders in most cases, can get a visa on arrival at Tribhuvan International Airport by paying a fee in cash or card and filling an online pre-registration form; Indian citizens actually need no visa at all to enter Nepal (only a valid photo ID), a longstanding open-border arrangement between the two countries.
averageFlightTime: ~9.7h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Kathmandu?
faqs.answer: Most foreign nationals, including Indian passport holders in most cases, can get a visa on arrival at Tribhuvan International Airport by paying a fee in cash or card and filling an online pre-registration form; Indian citizens actually need no visa at all to enter Nepal (only a valid photo ID), a longstanding open-border arrangement between the two countries.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Kathmandu?
faqs.answer: October-November and March-April for clear mountain views and comfortable highs of 22-25°C; the June-August monsoon brings heavy rain and obscured Himalayan views. Subtropical highland climate moderated by the Kathmandu Valley's elevation (~1,400m): warm summers (highs 28-30°C) with a heavy June-September monsoon, and cool, dry, clear winters (highs 18-20°C, chilly nights near 2-5°C).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Kathmandu safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Kathmandu is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Kathmandu?
faqs.answer: The local currency is NPR. Carry some cash for smaller vendors — card acceptance can be inconsistent outside major hotels and malls.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Mia Müller
reviews.title: Beautiful destination, well organized
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Narayanhiti Palace Museum grounds and Pashupatinath Temple. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Priya Ibrahim
reviews.title: A little rushed but still wonderful
reviews.body: Booked this as a couple trip and it was well paced. Swayambhunath (Monkey Temple) in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Olivia Garcia
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: Our trip to Kathmandu was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Narayanhiti Palace Museum grounds was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Tom Santos
reviews.title: Loved every moment
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Pashupatinath Temple and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Sarah Andersen
reviews.title: Beautiful destination, well organized
reviews.body: Kathmandu is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Ratna Park. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Sofia van Dijk
